why_v1_does_not_prove_world_model_failur: Target cache contains only 6 items, so prototype statistics are unstable and evaluation covers too few valid items.
Head-only training froze Stage2 semantic backbone, so the model could not adapt semantic representations.
Free-rollout evaluation used only 2 valid items, making zero top5 accuracy inconclusive.
Prototype vocabulary is under-sampled (mean 4.08 samples/prototype), so many classes are effectively noise.
This was explicitly a smoke test (100 steps) and not a controlled semantic-branch training run.
why_next_step_needs_large_cache_and_sema: Larger target cache stabilizes prototype counts and raises the effective sample-per-prototype budget.
Controlled unfreeze lets the semantic branch learn to align hidden state with prototype targets.
Head-only zero top5 accuracy suggests representation mismatch rather than a failed world model objective.
Bigger cache enables meaningful frequency baselines and non-trivial free-rollout comparisons.
Semantic branch unfreeze is required to test world-model signal without touching Stage1 or trace dynamics.
